paper.title: The Walkaway Star HP Tau/G2: Evidence for a Stellar Merger

extraction.summary: The paper discusses walkaway stars (G2, KPNO 15, G1) ejected from the young HP Tau stellar group at velocities of 5-17 km/s. These are cluster escapers, not Galactic-unbound HVS candidates. No object is presented as possibly unbound from the Milky Way.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Young Stars in the HP Tau Group, containing three walkaway stars (KPNO 15, HP Tau/G1, HP Tau/G2) flagged in the Notes column.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as walkaway stars ejected from a young stellar group at velocities of 5-17 km/s. Walkaway stars are cluster escapers with velocities between 5 and 30 km/s (de Mink et al. 2014), not Galactic-unbound HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: Definition of walkaway stars as velocities between 5 and 30 km/s
Four stars (G1, G2, KPNO 15, HP Tau) are identified as known or suspected walkaway stars
Table 1: Young Stars in the HP Tau Group with walkaway star annotations

candidate_groups_considered.description: Proper Motions within Rest Frame and Radial and Space Velocities for HP Tau group members.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The velocities reported (tangential velocities 5.65-8.44 km/s, space velocities ~17.5 km/s) are characteristic of walkaway stars ejected from a young stellar group. The paper does not discuss any object as possibly unbound from the Milky Way potential.

source_refs.context: Tangential velocity of G2 (5.65 km/s) and KPNO 15 (8.44 km/s)
G1 has a tangential velocity of 8.5 km/s and space velocity of about 17.5 km/s
